subject: Public Health
title: Schizophrenia Demystified
description: A micro-lesson that makes Schizophrenia Demystified usable.

hook.text: Schizophrenia doesn't mean 'split personality'—that's a myth. It means a split from reality.

buildup.text: Hallucinations, delusions, and disorganized thinking affect about 24 million people worldwide.

discovery.text: Schizophrenia involves excess dopamine in some brain pathways and too little in others.

twist.text: With proper medication and support, many people with schizophrenia live independently and hold jobs.

climax.text: Early intervention in the first psychotic episode dramatically improves long-term outcomes.

punchline.text: Schizophrenia is manageable. Misunderstanding it is the real danger.

quiz.question: What does schizophrenia actually involve?
options: A split from reality with hallucinations and delusions
